title: FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Data)
publisher: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis
description: The St. Louis Fed's economic data service. Authoritative mirror of the BLS Average Price (AP) program: the current U.S. city-average retail price for hundreds of food and household commodities (e.g. tomatoes, ground beef, coffee, eggs, milk), keyed by BLS/FRED series id. Prefer this over generic web search to benchmark what an item costs nationally on average. Each item's series id must be resolved first (curated bls_series_id attribute) — this source reads the latest observation for that series.

example_asks: national average price of Roma tomatoes
what is the current national average retail price of ground beef
benchmark our coffee invoice price against the national average
how much do boneless chicken breasts cost on average in the US
latest national average price for a dozen large eggs

tos_note: Public U.S. government / Federal Reserve data. Free FRED API key required (register at https://fredaccount.stlouisfed.org/apikeys). FRED mirrors BLS Average Price (AP) series; ~120 requests/min per key. Per-series id hosting must be verified — some BLS AP series may be hosted under a different FRED id or not at all (run fred_smoke.py).

params.name: series_id
params.description: FRED/BLS series id for this item, e.g. 'APU0000712311' (Tomatoes, field grown). Bound from the entity's curated bls_series_id attribute — the stage-1 series-resolution map in item_series_map.json.
